characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: His superhero form isSuper Why, who has the ability to change words in a sentence to adjust its meaning. Affectionate Nickname: He sometimes goes by "Why". Becomes a plot point in "The Boy Who Cried Wolf", where "Why" is Baby Joy's first word. Ambiguously Brown: He has olive skin and his race is unknown, although it's speculated by fans that he could be Hispanic or Latino. Birthday Episode: He has his birthday in "The Twelve Dancing Princesses," wherein his family are acting sneaky because they were planning a surprise party for him. Blonde, Brunette, Redhead: The Brunet to Woofster's Blond and Red's Redhead.

characters.name: Whyatt/Super Why
characters.description: His superhero form isSuper Why, who has the ability to change words in a sentence to adjust its meaning. Affectionate Nickname: He sometimes goes by "Why". Becomes a plot point in "The Boy Who Cried Wolf", where "Why" is Baby Joy's first word. Ambiguously Brown: He has olive skin and his race is unknown, although it's speculated by fans that he could be Hispanic or Latino. Birthday Episode: He has his birthday in "The Twelve Dancing Princesses," wherein his family are acting sneaky because they were planning a surprise party for him. Blonde, Brunette, Redhead: The Brunet to Woofster's Blond and Red's Redhead.

characters.name: Red/Wonder Red
characters.description: Her superhero form isWonder Red, who has the ability to swap words with ones that rhyme ("weed" into "seed", "wall" into "ball"). Adaptational Modesty: InComic Book Adventures, her outfit was changed so as to cover her arms and legs. Blonde, Brunette, Redhead: The Redhead to Woofster's Blond and Whyatt/Princess Pea's Brunet(te). Braids of Action: As Wonder Red, she wears her hair in braids. Catchphrase: When she arrives at the Book Club, she says "Red Riding Hood, rolling in!"

characters.name: Pig/Alpha-Pig
characters.description: His superhero form isAlpha-Pig, who has the ability to create objects by using the alphabet. The Baby of the Bunch: He is considered the youngest of the Super Readers, but is treated as an equal. He is also the youngest of his brothers, but they tend to believe that he's too young to do certain things and often ignore him. Brainy Pig: He shows great architectural know-how, as well as being proficient with the alphabet. The Cameo: Despite not appearing inComic Book Adventures, he makes a cameo as a doll in "Outta Compound Words." Catchphrase:When he arrives at the Book Club, he says "P is for Pig!"When the viewers help him, he says "Let's give ourselves a big thumbs up!"

characters.name: Princess Pea/Princess Presto
characters.description: Princess Peais a secondary protagonist. She is a young, dark-skinned girl who loves dancing. Her main claim to fame is being the daughter of the princess fromThe Princess and the Pea. Her superhero form isPrincess Presto, who, like Alpha-Pig, can create objects, however she writes out the letters rather than using the alphabet. Alliterative Name:PrincessPea andPrincessPresto. Blonde, Brunette, Redhead: The Brunette to Woofster's Blond and Red's Redhead. Catchphrase:When she arrives at the Book Club, she says "Princess Pea at your service!"When she summons books, she says "Peas and carrots, carrots and peas, book come out, please please please!"

characters.name: Puppy/Woofster
characters.description: Woofster, formerly known asPuppy, is the deuteragonist of the show. He is a Golden Retriever puppy who lives with Whyatt. His main claim to fame is appearing in an in-universe series of children's books. His superhero form is...well,Woofster, who has the ability to define words using his Doggy Dictionary. Blonde, Brunette, Redhead: The Blond to Whyatt/Princess Pea's Brunet(te) and Red's Redhead. The Cameo: Despite being removed fromComic Book Adventures, he made a cameo as a painting Super Why made in "Power Paige's Art Smarts." A Dog Named "Dog": In Season 2 at least, where he is called "Puppy" when he is in his normal form. Averted in Season 3 as he is called "Woofster", even in his normal form.

characters.name: Power Paige
characters.description: Power Paigeis the newest member of the Super Readers, introduced in the 2023 short-form seriesSuper Why! Comic Book Adventures. Unlike the other characters, she doesn't seem to come from a fairytale. Her specialty is correcting word problems with her Power Pencil. Alliterative Name:PowerPaige. Punny Name: Paige, as in the pages of a book.

characters.name: Jill
characters.description: Jill is a resident of Storybrook Village. She is from the nursery rhyme "Jack and Jill". Adapted Out: We never see Jack, we only see Jill. However, this is justified, since there was already a Jack and they probably didn't want to break theOne-Steve Limit. Bratty Half-Pint: In "The Three Little Pigs" when she kept knocking Pig's blocks over. She gets better after this episode, though. Curtains Match the Window: Her brown hair matches her brown eyes. Girlish Pigtails: Puts her hair up in these.

characters.name: Jack Beanstalk
characters.description: Jack, known for climbing the beanstalk, is Whyatt and Joy's teenage brother. Berserk Button: Whyatt being in his room, though if you've seen "Goldilocks and the Three Bears", you can understand why. Cool Big Bro: His defining trait. Jerkass Ball: In a rare out-of-character moment, "Goldilocks and the Three Bears: The Mystery" has him quickly blame Whyatt for breaking his guitar string, even though he had no evidence.The actual culprit was Baby Joy. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: He can sometimes be mad at Whyatt or accuse him of something but quickly forgives him.

characters.name: Joy Beanstalk
characters.description: Joy is Jack and Whyatt's baby sister. Baby Talk: Other than babbling/crying, she can say'Why' and less commonly, 'Why-why','No', 'light', 'off', and 'sit'. Goo-Goo-Godlike:In "Jack and the Beanstalk", she grows huge while throwing a fit. However, this was likely a figment of Whyatt's imagination.In "Goldilocks and the Three Bears: The Mystery",she broke Jack's guitar string by...plucking it. In "Jack and the Beanstalk", she grows huge while throwing a fit. However, this was likely a figment of Whyatt's imagination. In "Goldilocks and the Three Bears: The Mystery",she broke Jack's guitar string by...plucking it.

characters.name: Peter Piper
characters.description: Peter Piper is a resident of Storybrook Village. He is from the eponymous nursery rhyme. Alliterative Name:PeterPiper. Berserk Button:Alwaysask before you take one of his peppers. Red had the misfortune of learning this the hard way in "Hansel and Gretel". Token Minority: He has dark skin.

characters.name: Sleeping Beauty
characters.description: Sleeping Beauty is a resident of Storybrook Village. She is from the eponymous fairy tale. Race Lift: She is usually depicted as white in other animated tellings of her story, most notablythe 1959 Disney film.She is Asian in this series; however, her exact ethnicity remains in mystery. True Blue Femininity/Graceful Ladies Like Purple: Her crown and dress are mostly blue with some purple accents.

characters.name: Wolfy
characters.description: Wolfy is a resident of Storybrook Village. He is the son of the Big Bad Wolf. Bratty Half-Pint: From time to time.

characters.name: Little Boy Blue
characters.description: Little Boy Blue is a resident of Storybrook Village. He is from the eponymous nursery rhyme. Token Minority: He looks vaguely Asian.

characters.name: Pig's Brothers
characters.description: Middle Pig and Biggest Pig are Littlest Pig's older brothers. Color-Coded Characters: Middle Pig wears red, and Biggest Pig wears green. Expy: Their colors make them look a lot likeMario and Luigi.

characters.name: King and Queen Pea
characters.description: King and Queen Pea are Princess Pea's parents.

characters.name: Aiden
characters.description: Aiden is the protagonist of the book "The Rhyming Carnival". He does not live in Storybrook Village. Expy: He looks (and sort of acts) identical toAlex Porter, right down to sharing the same voice actor.

characters.name: More Man
characters.description: Affably Evil: Despite being a villain, and a bit petty at times, he's also pretty friendly,even to the viewers. Alliterative Name: Both 'More' and 'Man' start with 'M.' Amazing Technicolor Population: His skin is purple inComic Book Adventures. Brought to You by the Letter "S": His belt has the letter M on it, and he wears an M-shaped mask. InComic Book Adventures,his head is shaped like the letter. Evil Is Petty: In "More Man's More Plan," he gets rid of periods because he doesn't want his sentences to end.

characters.name: The Eraser
characters.description: Affably Evil: He can be polite to the Super Readers, and to the viewer as well. In "Gotta Get the Letter G," when he realizes that he is alone at the park, he invites Power Paige inside, and in "Cats at Bat," he plays baseball with Paige and Red. Amazing Technicolor Population: He has pink skin. Brought to You by the Letter "S": He has the letter E on his shirt inComic Book Adventures. Didn't Think This Through: In "Gotta Get the Letter G," he erases the letter G from the park gate so he can have the park all to himself. but then he realizes that he cannot do anything at the park because he is by himself. Then, he cannot get out of the park because he erased the G. Evil Is Hammy: He loves to ham it up.

characters.name: The Shuffler
characters.description: Affably Evil: She was friendly with Power Paige in "Outta Space is Outta Control," and helped her save fellow villain Outta Space. Big "NO!": She performed this when Super Why and Power Paige retrieve their paintings from her. Brought to You by the Letter "S": A subtle example; the curl on her forehead makes the letter 'S' for Shuffler. Cartoon Juggling: She shuffles things up by juggling them, using the shower variant. Extendable Arms: She extends her arms to steal one of Super Why's paintings in "Power Paige's Art Smarts."

characters.name: Outta Space
characters.description: Creepy High-Pitched Voice: His voice was digitally pitched up quite a lot, and he's one of the villains. Distressed Dude:In "Reader Valley Freeze," he accidentally froze himself, and Power Paige had to use the word "hot" to unfreeze him.In "Outta Space is Outta Control," his flying saucer goes out of control, and it's up to Paige and The Shuffler to save him. In "Reader Valley Freeze," he accidentally froze himself, and Power Paige had to use the word "hot" to unfreeze him. In "Outta Space is Outta Control," his flying saucer goes out of control, and it's up to Paige and The Shuffler to save him. Flying Saucer: He uses his saucer to zap spaces from sentences.
